S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — TISSUE STILL MATTERS: BRAF V600E in melanoma vs colorectal — same mutation, profoundly different biology

data.question: Same BRAF V600E mutation. Does BRAF + MEK inhibition work?

branches.label: Melanoma
branches.detail: Response rate above 60%. EGFR feedback signaling is absent. BRAF + MEK inhibition is durable.
branches.fix: Tumor-agnostic approval holds. Strong, durable response.

branches.label: Colorectal cancer
branches.detail: Weak single-agent BRAF inhibitor response. Abundant EGFR signaling provides a bypass route. BRAF blockade is circumvented.
branches.fix: Must add anti-EGFR antibody. The tissue that was supposed to be incidental turns out to matter.

resolver.label: 'Agnostic' in the label does not mean uniform in the biology
resolver.detail: Feedback reactivation of EGFR restores the signal the drug blocked. Tissue context changes the mechanism.

S09.meta: // SLIDE 09 — PRIMARY RESISTANCE: KRAS G12C inhibitor response rate vs EGFR/ALK — oncogene addiction explains the gap

data.axisLabel: Objective response rate in lung adenocarcinoma

S10.meta: // SLIDE 10 — ONCOGENE ADDICTION: single-driver cancers respond; multi-driver cancers resist

data.startLabel: Precision oncology: match the alteration, prescribe the drug
data.splitLabel: How many drivers does the cancer depend on?

tracks.label: Single-driver, oncogene-addicted
tracks.outcome: Dramatic, durable responses — sometimes approaching cure
notes: CML with BCR-ABL: imatinib response rate above 90% — chronic management
NTRK fusion-positive: larotrectinib response above 75%, potentially curative
Cancer concentrated survival dependency on one driver — limited escape options

tracks.label: Multi-driver, microenvironment-dominated
tracks.outcome: Resistance despite matching — no dominant dependency to block
notes: Pancreatic adenocarcinoma: KRAS + TP53 + SMAD4 + CDKN2A — none cleanly druggable
GBM: many alterations, no dominant single dependency
TNBC: heterogeneous drivers, multiple active pathways simultaneously
